title: Trust Thyself
source: Ralph Waldo Emerson, Essays: First Series, Self-Reliance (Project Gutenberg #2944)
why_selected: A compact American statement of inner authority; good for appositions, imperatives, and abstract nouns.

glosses.hint: people living at the same time

grammar: Two imperatives set the passage's command structure: Trust, Accept.
The long third sentence hangs on Great men have done + and confided + betraying.
Working and predominating are participial continuations, not new finite verbs.
codex_comment_zh: 这不是普通的“相信自己”。Emerson 把自我信任写成一种身体会共鸣的内在命令：heart vibrates to that iron string。
codex_comment_en: The passage converts self-trust from advice into resonance: the heart answers an iron string.

segments.text_en: Trust thyself: every heart vibrates to that iron string.
segments.component_zh: 信任[祈使] 你自己：每一颗心 震动[一般现在] 到 那根 铁弦。
segments.codex_comment: 先抓住命令 spine: Trust thyself. 冒号后面不是解释词义，而是把命令变成图像。

segments.text_en: Accept the place the divine providence has found for you, the society of your contemporaries, the connection of events.
segments.component_zh: 接受[祈使] 那个位置，神意 已经 找到[现在完成 has+pp] 给你，接受[省略祈使] 你的同时代人的社会，接受[省略祈使] 事件的连接。
segments.codex_comment: Accept 连续带三个宾语：place, society, connection。它不是消极接受，是先承认你已经站在这里。

palette.usage: present perfect inside relative-like object phrase
object in a list after Accept

segments.text_en: Great men have always done so, and confided themselves childlike to the genius of their age, betraying their perception that the absolutely trustworthy was seated at their heart, working through their hands, predominating in all their being.
segments.component_zh: 伟大的人 总是 已经 这样 做过[现在完成 have+pp]，并且 把他们自己 像孩子一样 托付[一般过去] 给 他们时代的天才，显露[现在分词 -ing] 他们的感知：那个绝对可信的东西 坐落[一般过去 被动感] 在 他们的心里，工作[现在分词 -ing] 通过 他们的手，在 他们全部存在中 占主导[现在分词 -ing]。
segments.codex_comment: Great men have always done so 把个人直觉接到历史维度；done so 指前面的 accept/confide。

palette.usage: participial phrase explaining the prior clause

text_en: Trust thyself: every heart vibrates to that iron string. Accept the place the divine providence has found for you, the society of your contemporaries, the connection of events. Great men have always done so, and confided themselves childlike to the genius of their age, betraying their perception that the absolutely trustworthy was seated at their heart, working through their hands, predominating in all their being.

title: A Foolish Consistency
source: Ralph Waldo Emerson, Essays: First Series, Self-Reliance (Project Gutenberg #2944)
why_selected: A sharp passage about changing one's mind; useful for aphorism, concession, and contrast.

glosses.hint: a frightening little spirit; here, a mental bugbear

grammar: The first sentence is an aphorism: subject + is + metaphor.
Adored by... is a reduced passive relative clause modifying hobgoblin.
Though introduces permission for contradiction, not an apology for error.
codex_comment_zh: 这段在说：不要为了维持昨天的自我形象而背叛今天真实看见的东西。foolish consistency 是对死一致性的讽刺，不是反对诚实。
codex_comment_en: This is not permission to be sloppy; it is an attack on loyalty to a dead version of yourself.

segments.text_en: A foolish consistency is the hobgoblin of little minds, adored by little statesmen and philosophers and divines.
segments.component_zh: 一种 愚蠢的 一致性 是[一般现在] 小心灵的 妖怪，被 小政治家、小哲学家和神学家 崇拜[过去分词 被动修饰]。
segments.codex_comment: A foolish consistency is... 是一句 aphorism：抽象名词 + is + 怪物隐喻，直接下判决。

segments.text_en: With consistency a great soul has simply nothing to do.
segments.component_zh: 对于 一致性，一个 伟大的灵魂 简直 没有什么 要做[一般现在 has nothing to do]。
segments.codex_comment: With consistency 放句首，像中文的“至于一致性”。注意不是 for consistency。

palette.usage: has nothing to do with structure

segments.text_en: He may as well concern himself with his shadow on the wall.
segments.component_zh: 他 也完全可以 让自己 操心[情态 may + 原形] 墙上的影子。
segments.codex_comment: may as well 是 dismissive: 如果你真纠结这种一致性，那你还不如去管墙上的影子。

segments.text_en: Speak what you think now in hard words and to-morrow speak what to-morrow thinks in hard words again, though it contradict every thing you said to-day.
segments.component_zh: 说出[祈使] 你 现在 想的东西，用 强硬的话；并且 明天 再说出[祈使] 明天 所想的东西，用 强硬的话，即使 它 矛盾[一般现在] 你 今天 说过[一般过去] 的一切。
segments.codex_comment: Speak what you think now... 这里的 hard words 是不把真实想法磨软。

text_en: A foolish consistency is the hobgoblin of little minds, adored by little statesmen and philosophers and divines. With consistency a great soul has simply nothing to do. He may as well concern himself with his shadow on the wall. Speak what you think now in hard words and to-morrow speak what to-morrow thinks in hard words again, though it contradict every thing you said to-day.

title: Rejected Thoughts Return
source: Ralph Waldo Emerson, Essays: First Series, Self-Reliance (Project Gutenberg #2944)
why_selected: This is the doorway to Emerson: the thought you dismissed returns from another mind with majesty.
context_note: Before the famous commands, Emerson explains why private perception matters: your rejected thought may come back wearing another author’s authority.

glosses.hint: a sense of grandeur that feels distant or estranged
cheerful stubbornness; unyielding but not bitter
the general opinion or public outcry

glosses.word: take with shame our own opinion from another
glosses.hint: accept back one's own idea from someone else, feeling embarrassed
glosses.chinese: 羞愧地从别人那里取回自己的观点

vocab_targets: take with shame our own opinion from another
grammar: 一般现在时用于表达普遍真理或事实，如 'we recognize', 'they come back', 'Great works have'。
比较结构 'no more ... than' 表示 '和……一样不'，这里强调没有比这更动人的教训。
时间状语从句 'when the whole cry of voices is on the other side' 使用一般现在时表示将来。
将来时 'will say' 和 'shall be forced' 表示对未来的预测。
codex_comment_zh: 这段很适合你：它说被你丢掉的想法，会从陌生人的话里带着陌生的庄严回来。真正的学习不是识别，而是认领。
codex_comment_en: The important movement is return: your rejected thought comes back with alienated majesty.

segments.text_en: In every work of genius we recognize our own rejected thoughts; they come back to us with a certain alienated majesty.
segments.component_zh: 在每一件[定语从句起点] genius 的作品中，我们 认出[一般现在] 我们自己的 被拒绝的 thoughts；它们 回来[一般现在] 到我们 带着 一种 疏远的 majesty。
segments.codex_comment: There is a time... 先给出普遍经验：每个人都会发现 jealousy is ignorance。

palette.chinese: 在每一件天才的作品中
我们自己的被拒绝的想法
palette.usage: 宾语，rejected 是过去分词作定语
方式状语，修饰 come back

segments.text_en: Great works of art have no more affecting lesson for us than this.
segments.component_zh: 伟大的 艺术作品 有[一般现在] 没有 更 动人的 lesson 对于 我们 比 这个。
segments.codex_comment: The eye was placed where one ray should fall... 是强图像：你的视角不是错误，是你的位置。

palette.english: have no more affecting lesson for us than this
palette.chinese: 没有比这更动人的教训给我们

segments.text_en: They teach us to abide by our spontaneous impression with good-humored inflexibility then most when the whole cry of voices is on the other side.
segments.component_zh: 它们 教[一般现在] 我们 坚持[不定式] 我们的 自发的 impression 带着 好脾气的 inflexibility 在 最 时候 当 整个 声音的呼喊 是[一般现在] 在 另一边。
segments.codex_comment: In every work of genius... 句子很长，但核心是 we recognize our own rejected thoughts。

palette.usage: 方式状语，修饰 abide by

segments.text_en: Else to-morrow a stranger will say with masterly good sense precisely what we have thought and felt all the time, and we shall be forced to take with shame our own opinion from another.
segments.component_zh: 否则 明天 一个 陌生人 将说[一般将来] 带着 大师般的 好 sense 精确地 什么 我们 已经[过去完成] 想 和 感觉 所有 时间，并且 我们 将被迫[一般将来被动] 带着 羞愧 接受 我们自己的 意见 从 另一个。
segments.codex_comment: They come back to us... 注意 alienated majesty：想法因为从别人嘴里回来，所以显得陌生又威严。

palette.english: we have thought and felt all the time
we shall be forced to take

text_en: In every work of genius we recognize our own rejected thoughts; they come back to us with a certain alienated majesty. Great works of art have no more affecting lesson for us than this. They teach us to abide by our spontaneous impression with good-humored inflexibility then most when the whole cry of voices is on the other side. Else to-morrow a stranger will say with masterly good sense precisely what we have thought and felt all the time, and we shall be forced to take with shame our own opinion from another.

title: Whoso Would Be a Man
source: Ralph Waldo Emerson, Essays: First Series, Self-Reliance (Project Gutenberg #2944)
why_selected: The central command of the essay: integrity over borrowed goodness.
context_note: This comes after Emerson attacks social conformity; the question is whether goodness is real or only a name enforced by others.

glosses.hint: person who does not conform to established customs
symbol of victory or honor that lasts forever
glosses.chinese: 不朽的棕榈叶（象征荣誉）

glosses.hint: state of being whole and undivided
set free from guilt or responsibility

grammar: 情态动词 would 表示愿望，must 表示必要性，must not 表示禁止。
被动语态：must not be hindered by...，结构为情态动词 + be + 过去分词。
虚拟语气：if it be goodness 中 be 为古语虚拟式，现代英语用 is。
祈使句：Absolve you to yourself，动词原形开头，you 为宾语。
codex_comment_zh: 这段是 Self-Reliance 的锋刃：成为一个人，不是先被喜欢，而是不被 conformity 吞掉。
codex_comment_en: Whoso would be a man must be a nonconformist: the sentence makes manhood a refusal before it is an identity.

segments.text_en: Whoso would be a man, must be a nonconformist.
segments.component_zh: 无论谁[情态动词 would] 想要成为[不定式 to be] 一个人, 必须[情态动词 must] 是[be] 一个不墨守成规者. 他[关系代词 who] 想要[情态动词 would] 收集[不定式 to gather] 不朽的棕榈叶, 不能[情态动词 must not] 被阻碍[被动 be hindered] 被[by] 善良的名字, 而[but] 必须[情态动词 must] 探索[if 从句] 它是否[if] 是[be] 善良. 没有东西[否定] 是[be] 最终神圣的[at last sacred] 除了[but] 你自己思想的完整性. 赦免[祈使句 absolve] 你自己[to yourself], 并且[and] 你[shall] 将拥有[have] 世界的赞同.
segments.codex_comment: Whoso would be a man 是古典/庄严句式，意思是 whoever wants to be fully human。

palette.usage: 古语，相当于 whoever，引导主语从句
情态动词 would + be，表示愿望
泛指真正的人，有男子气概的人
情态动词 must + be，表示必要性
名词，指不遵从常规的人

segments.text_en: He who would gather immortal palms must not be hindered by the name of goodness, but must explore if it be goodness.
segments.codex_comment: must be a nonconformist 把身份定义成动作：不顺从。

palette.usage: 关系代词 who 引导定语从句，修饰 he
情态动词 would + gather，表示愿望
象征胜利或荣誉，palms 用复数
情态动词 must not + be + 过去分词，被动语态
介词短语，表示被...所阻碍
连词，表示转折，连接两个并列成分
if 引导的宾语从句，be 为虚拟语气（古语）

segments.text_en: Nothing is at last sacred but the integrity of your own mind.
segments.codex_comment: Nothing is at last sacred but... 结构是排除法：最后神圣的只剩 integrity of your own mind。

palette.usage: 连词，表示转折，连接两个并列成分
系表结构，at last 为状语

palette.english: the integrity of your own mind
palette.usage: 名词短语，of 表示所属

segments.text_en: Absolve you to yourself, and you shall have the suffrage of the world.
segments.codex_comment: Absolve you to yourself 是宗教/legal 语感：先在自己面前被赦免，才会有世界的认可。

palette.usage: 祈使句，absolve 为及物动词，to yourself 为间接宾语
情态动词 shall 表示承诺或必然结果
名词短语，suffrage 原指投票权，此处指支持

text_en: Whoso would be a man, must be a nonconformist. He who would gather immortal palms must not be hindered by the name of goodness, but must explore if it be goodness. Nothing is at last sacred but the integrity of your own mind. Absolve you to yourself, and you shall have the suffrage of the world.
